---
title: "CustomImageDataset Arguments Explained"
---

## Constructor Arguments

| Argument | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| annotations_file | str or Path | вң… Yes | Path to CSV mapping filenames вҶ’ labels |
| img_dir | str or Path | вң… Yes | Directory containing the image files |
| transform | Callable or None | вқҢ Optional | Function applied to image (feature) |
| target_transform | Callable or None | вқҢ Optional | Function applied to label (target) |

---

## 1. annotations_file вҖ” the CSV

A path to a CSV file with 2 columns, no header assumed (since the code uses iloc[idx, 0] and iloc[idx, 1]):

```text
annotations.csv
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ filename в”Ӯ label в”Ӯ вҶҗ column 0, column 1
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ tshirt0001.jpg в”Ӯ 0 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ trouser0002.jpg в”Ӯ 1 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ sneaker0003.jpg в”Ӯ 7 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ ... в”Ӯ ... в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Shape: (N, 2) where N = number of samples.

---

## 2. img_dir вҖ” the image folder

A directory path. Filenames inside must match column 0 of the CSV:

```text
/data/fashion/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ annotations.csv
в””в”Җв”Җ images/ вҶҗ this is img_dir
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ tshirt0001.jpg
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ trouser0002.jpg
 в””в”Җв”Җ sneaker0003.jpg
```

Inside __getitem__, the full path is built as:

```python
img_path = img_dir + "/" + img_labels.iloc[idx, 0]
 = "/data/fashion/images" + "/" + "tshirt0001.jpg"
```

---

## 3. transform вҖ” image preprocessing

A callable that takes a tensor image and returns a transformed tensor.

- Input shape (from decode_image): (C, H, W) вҖ” channels-first, uint8, values [0, 255]
- Output shape: depends on transforms (commonly (C, H', W'), float32, values [0, 1])

Typical use:

```python
from torchvision import transforms

transform = transforms.Compose([
 transforms.Resize((224, 224)), # (C, H, W) вҶ’ (C, 224, 224)
 transforms.ConvertImageDtype(torch.float32), # uint8 вҶ’ float32, scale to [0,1]
 transforms.Normalize(mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406],
 std =[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]),
])
```

---

## 4. target_transform вҖ” label preprocessing

A callable applied to the label. Useful for one-hot encoding, type casting, etc.

```python
import torch
from torch.nn.functional import one_hot

# Convert integer label вҶ’ one-hot tensor of length 10
target_transform = lambda y: one_hot(torch.tensor(y), num_classes=10)
```

---

## Data Flow Diagram

```text
 annotations.csv img_dir/
 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
 в–ј в–ј
 в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
 в”Ӯ img_labelsв”Ӯ в”Ӯ .jpg files в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ DataFrame в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
 в”Ӯ (N, 2) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
 iloc[idx,0]в”Җв”Җв”җ iloc[idx,0] used to build path
 в–ј в”Ӯ
 filename в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә join
 в”Ӯ
 в–ј
 decode_image()
 в”Ӯ
 в–ј
 image tensor (C,H,W) uint8
 в”Ӯ
 transform (optional)
 в”Ӯ
 в–ј
 image tensor (C,H',W') float32

 iloc[idx,1] в”Җв–ә label (int) в”Җв–ә target_transform в”Җв–ә label tensor
 в”Ӯ
 в–ј
 return (image, label)
```

---

## Complete Example

```python
import torch
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from torchvision import transforms
from torch.nn.functional import one_hot

# 1. Define transforms
image_transform = transforms.Compose([
 transforms.Resize((28, 28)),
 transforms.ConvertImageDtype(torch.float32),
])

label_transform = lambda y: one_hot(torch.tensor(y), num_classes=10).float()

# 2. Instantiate dataset
dataset = CustomImageDataset(
 annotations_file="/data/fashion/annotations.csv",
 img_dir="/data/fashion/images",
 transform=image_transform,
 target_transform=label_transform,
)

# 3. Inspect one sample
img, label = dataset[0]
print(img.shape) # torch.Size([3, 28, 28]) or [1, 28, 28] if grayscale
print(label.shape) # torch.Size([10]) one-hot vector

# 4. Wrap in DataLoader for batching
loader = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=64, shuffle=True)
for batch_imgs, batch_labels in loader:
 print(batch_imgs.shape) # torch.Size([64, 3, 28, 28])
 print(batch_labels.shape) # torch.Size([64, 10])
 break
```

---

## Minimal Example (no transforms)

```python
dataset = CustomImageDataset(
 annotations_file="annotations.csv",
 img_dir="images/",
)
img, label = dataset[0]
print(img.dtype, img.shape) # torch.uint8 torch.Size([3, H, W])
print(type(label)) # <class 'numpy.int64'> вҶҗ raw from pandas
```

> рҹ’Ў Without target_transform, the label is whatever pandas returns (usually np.int64). You'll typically want at least torch.tensor(label) to feed into a loss function.
